title: Nvidia Q3 FY2026 Financial Results
content: SANTA CLARA, Calif. — Nvidia today reported revenue for the third quarter ended October 27, 2025, of $35.1 billion, up 94% from a year ago and up 17% from the previous quarter. GAAP earnings per diluted share for the quarter were $0.78, up 111% from a year ago and up 16% from the previous quarter. Non-GAAP earnings per diluted share were $0.81, up 103% from a year ago and up 14% from the previous quarter. Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of Nvidia, said: 'The age of AI is in full steam, propelling a global shift to Nvidia computing. Demand for Blackwell is amazing as reasoning AI adds another scaling law — increasing inference-time compute — for frontier model developers.' Data Center revenue was $30.8 billion, up 112% year-on-year, reflecting hyperscaler demand for H100, H200, and new Blackwell B200 GPUs. Gaming revenue was $3.3 billion, up 15%. Professional Visualization was $0.5 billion, up 17%. Automotive was $0.45 billion, up 72% as autonomous driving programs scale. Gross margin was 74.6% GAAP and 75.0% non-GAAP. Nvidia repurchased 69 million shares for $8.4 billion during the quarter and declared a $0.01 per share quarterly cash dividend. The company guided Q4 FY2026 revenue to $37.5 billion, plus or minus 2%.
